title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not allow all requests. Enable explicit authorization.
input_row.rationale: Kubelets, by default, allow all authenticated requests (even anonymous ones) without needing explicit authorization checks from the apiserver. You should restrict this behavior and only allow explicitly authorized requests.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that the --authorization-mode argument exists and is not set to AlwaysAllow.
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/kubelet file on each node and set the KUBELET_ARGS parameter to "--authorization-mode=Webhook": KUBELET_ARGS="--authorization-mode=Webhook" Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl restart kubelet.service Impact: Unauthorized requests will be denied. Default Value: By default, --authorization-mode argument is set to AlwaysAllow. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet/  151 | P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-authentication-authorization/#kubelet- authentication
input_row.impact: Unauthorized requests will be denied. Default Value: By default, --authorization-mode argument is set to AlwaysAllow.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet/  151 | P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-authentication-authorization/#kubelet- authentication

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires verification of the kubelet process argument --authorization-mode on each node.
- The kubelet's startup arguments (such as --authorization-mode) are not exposed via the Kubernetes API or kubectl; they are set at the OS/process level.
- The audit procedure involves running ps -ef | grep kubelet on each node, which requires host-level access.
- There is no Kubernetes API or kubectl command that can directly retrieve the kubelet process arguments or its configuration file contents from the control plane.
- Therefore, this check cannot be fully automated via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl and requires manual/host-level verification.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each node in the Kubernetes cluster.
2. Run the following command to list the kubelet process and its arguments:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep kubelet
   ```
3. Examine the output and locate the --authorization-mode argument.
4. Verify that --authorization-mode is present and is NOT set to AlwaysAllow. The recommended value is Webhook (e.g., --authorization-mode=Webhook).
5. If --authorization-mode is missing or set to AlwaysAllow, this is a finding (non-compliant).
6. Repeat for all nodes in the cluster to ensure consistent configuration.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
